race.name: Kobberløpet
race.tagline: Forty-three classic kilometers through Norway's copper country — where Arctic mountain spring meets half a century of mining-town grit.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations with warm drinks and Norwegian race food

climate.description: April above the Arctic Circle delivers spring skiing with long daylight — up to 16 hours by mid-April. Temperatures range from -8°C to 5°C. The mountain setting between Sulitjelma and Fauske provides reliable late-season snow. Coastal influence means variable conditions, from firm spring crust to soft corn snow.
challenges: Spring snow transitions — morning crust to afternoon slush
Arctic wind exposure on mountain passes
Wax selection critical for classic technique in variable spring conditions
Coastal weather can change rapidly — prepare for all conditions

course.primary: Point-to-point from the former copper mining town of Sulitjelma to Fauske on the Norwegian coast. The classic-only course traverses Arctic mountain terrain with significant climbing through birch forest and above the treeline. The descent to Fauske delivers a dramatic finish.
course.surface: Machine-groomed mountain trails through birch forest and above-treeline terrain
course.grooming: Norwegian standard — well-prepared classic tracks for the point-to-point route
features: Point-to-point from mountain mining town to coastal Fauske
Above the Arctic Circle at 67°N latitude
Named after the copper (kobber) mining heritage of Sulitjelma
Classic technique only — pure diagonal stride and double pole
Above-treeline sections with panoramic Arctic mountain views
April spring skiing with extended Arctic daylight

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A historic Arctic race with Ski Classics Challengers status, stunning mountain scenery, and strong competitive depth. Prestige 4 (founded 1968, nearly 60 years of history) and Ski Classics membership lift it above its modest field size.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 with score 67 — 1-tier promotion applied, already TIER 2 so no change

history.summary: Founded in 1968, Kobberløpet is named after the copper mining heritage of Sulitjelma, a former mining town nestled in the mountains of Nordland above the Arctic Circle. The race traverses the route between Sulitjelma and the coastal town of Fauske, a journey that miners and their families would have made on skis for generations. Now part of the Ski Classics Challengers series, Kobberløpet attracts both elite long-distance racers and citizen skiers seeking an authentic Arctic mountain experience.
notable_facts: Named after copper (kobber) — Sulitjelma was a major copper mining center from 1891 to 1991
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series
Above the Arctic Circle — one of the northernmost races in the Ski Classics network
Sulitjelma mines operated for exactly 100 years before closing in 1991
April timing offers up to 16 hours of daylight above the Arctic Circle
The point-to-point route from mountains to coast mirrors historic travel routes
Founded in 1968 — approaching 60 years of continuous racing
